title: Contributing to seerSurv
text: welcome contributions kinds — bug reports, documentation improvements, new features, additional cancer-type data.

title: Contributing to seerSurv
text: Please open issue GitHub include: minimal reproducible example (use reprex::reprex()). output sessionInfo(). exact error message unexpected output.

title: Contributing to seerSurv
text: Fork repository create branch main. Install development dependencies: Make changes. Follow tidyverse style guide. Document new functions roxygen2 tags run devtools::document(). Add update testthat tests. Aim > 90 % line coverage. Run full check suite confirm passes warnings notes: Submit pull request clear description changed .
code: install.packages(c("devtools", "roxygen2", "testthat", "covr", "lintr")) devtools::check()

title: Contributing to seerSurv
text: Indentation: 2 spaces (tabs). Line length: ≤ 100 characters. Avoid T / F — use TRUE / FALSE. Use \(x) lambda syntax (R ≥ 4.1) rather function(x) short anonymous functions. user-facing functions must complete roxygen2 documentation including least one @examples block. Internal helpers (prefixed .) require exported documentation inline comments.

title: Contributing to seerSurv
code: devtools::test() covr::package_coverage()

title: Multi-Tumour Analysis and Sensitivity Analyses with seerSurv
text: vignette demonstrates batch analysis workflow 11 cancer types bundled tumour_data_seer dataset across supported time horizons ("lifetime", "20y", "5y") information criteria ("AIC", "BIC").
code: library(seerSurv) library(dplyr) library(tidyr) library(ggplot2)

title: Multi-Tumour Analysis and Sensitivity Analyses with seerSurv
Multi-Tumour Analysis and Sensitivity Analyses with seerSurv
code: scenarios <- c("lifetime", "20y", "5y") criteria  <- c("AIC", "BIC")  run_batch <- function(scen, crit) {   tumour_data_seer |>     group_by(Tumor) |>     summarise(       out = list(         run_tumour_analysis(           tumour       = first(Tumor),           surv_vec_LR  = c(S0[Recurrence == "LR"], S1[Recurrence == "LR"],                            S2[Recurrence == "LR"], S3[Recurrence == "LR"],                            S4[Recurrence == "LR"], S5[Recurrence == "LR"]),           surv_vec_DR  = c(S0[Recurrence == "DR"], S1[Recurrence == "DR"],                            S2[Recurrence == "DR"], S3[Recurrence == "DR"],                            S4[Recurrence == "DR"], S5[Recurrence == "DR"]),           N_L          = first(N_LR),           N_D          = first(N_DR),           prop_male_LR = prop_male[Recurrence == "LR"],           mean_age_LR  = mean_age[Recurrence == "LR"],           prop_male_DR = prop_male[Recurrence == "DR"],           mean_age_DR  = mean_age[Recurrence == "DR"],           lifetable    = lifetable_seer,           scenario     = scen,           criterion    = crit         )       ),       .groups = "drop"     ) |>     unnest(out) |>     mutate(scenario = scen, criterion = crit) }  all_results <- bind_rows(   lapply(scenarios, function(s)     lapply(criteria, function(c) run_batch(s, c)) |> bind_rows()   ) )  dim(all_results) #> [1] 66 10

title: Multi-Tumour Analysis and Sensitivity Analyses with seerSurv
text: Base-case monthly transition probabilities (lifetime, AIC)
code: base <- all_results |>   filter(scenario == "lifetime", criterion == "AIC") |>   select(Tumor, P_LL, P_DL_approach2, P_Death_L_approach2, M_months)  knitr::kable(base, digits = 5,              caption = "Base-case monthly transition probabilities (lifetime, AIC)")

what: Sensitivity: P(DR) by scenario and criterion
title: Multi-Tumour Analysis and Sensitivity Analyses with seerSurv
text: P(DR) across scenarios criteria
code: all_results |>   filter(criterion == "AIC") |>   mutate(scenario = factor(scenario, levels = c("5y", "20y", "lifetime"))) |>   ggplot(aes(x = P_DL_approach2, y = reorder(Tumor, P_DL_approach2),              colour = scenario, shape = scenario)) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   labs(     title    = "P(DR) by Cancer Type and Time Horizon",     subtitle = "AIC-weighted model blend",     x        = "Monthly P(LR → DR)",     y        = NULL,     colour   = "Horizon",     shape    = "Horizon"   ) +   scale_colour_brewer(palette = "Set1") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Multi-Tumour Analysis and Sensitivity Analyses with seerSurv
text: AIC vs BIC — P(DR)
code: all_results |>   filter(scenario == "lifetime") |>   select(Tumor, criterion, P_DL_approach2) |>   pivot_wider(names_from = criterion, values_from = P_DL_approach2) |>   ggplot(aes(x = AIC, y = BIC, label = Tumor)) +   geom_point(size = 3, colour = "#E41A1C") +   geom_text(size = 3, vjust = -0.6, hjust = 0.5) +   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +   labs(     title = "AIC- vs BIC-weighted P(DR) — Lifetime Horizon",     x     = "P(LR → DR) under AIC weighting",     y     = "P(LR → DR) under BIC weighting"   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11)

title: Multi-Tumour Analysis and Sensitivity Analyses with seerSurv
code: # Export combined results to CSV write.csv(   all_results,   file      = "seerSurv_all_results.csv",   row.names = FALSE )  # Export base-case to Excel openxlsx::write.xlsx(   base,   file      = "seerSurv_basecase.xlsx",   sheetName = "Lifetime_AIC" )

title: Introduction to seerSurv: Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities
text: State-transition health economic models early-stage cancers often include local/regional recurrence (LR) state sits disease-free state distant recurrence (DR) death. Populating model requires three monthly transition probabilities LR: Trial-derived estimates rarely available clinical studies typically powered overall disease-free survival, LR follow-short observe full recurrence cascade. seerSurv fills gap deriving three probabilities aggregate (population-level) survival data extracted SEER-17 registry, covering ten cancer types.

title: Introduction to seerSurv: Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities
text: Pseudo-IPD construction — Aggregate survival proportions years 0–5 converted pseudo-individual patient data (prep_ipd()). Parametric modelling — Six distributions (exponential, Weibull, Gompertz, log-logistic, log-normal, gamma) fitted pseudo-IPD using weighted maximum likelihood via flexsurv (fit_models()). Model averaging — top-kk distributions AIC BIC receive relative-likelihood weights; final curve convex blend (compute_weights(), blend_survival()). Background mortality adjustment — Disease-specific survival multiplied age- sex-specific background survival US-CDC life-table obtain net (-cause) survival (make_background_surv()). Sojourn time PLLP_{\text{LL}} — mean LR sojourn time MM (months) equals area difference net LR net DR curves. PLLP_{\text{LL}} solves ∑t=0NPLLt=M\sum_{t=0}^{N} P_{\text{LL}}^t = M. Convolution PDLP_{\text{DL}} — modelled LR survival approximated sum patients still LR transitioned DR. PDLP_{\text{DL}} estimated least-squares (run_tumour_analysis()).

title: Introduction to seerSurv: Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities
text: Monthly transition probabilities — Melanoma (lifetime, AIC)
code: library(seerSurv) library(dplyr)  data(lifetable_seer)  # Five-year SEER-17 survival proportions — Melanoma s_lr <- c(1, 0.9959, 0.9886, 0.9831, 0.9783, 0.9754) s_dr <- c(1, 0.5491, 0.4396, 0.3945, 0.3630, 0.3410)  result <- run_tumour_analysis(   tumour       = "Melanoma",   surv_vec_LR  = s_lr,   surv_vec_DR  = s_dr,   N_L          = 64775L,   N_D          = 3121L,   prop_male_LR = 0.580,   mean_age_LR  = 61,   prop_male_DR = 0.692,   mean_age_DR  = 62,   lifetable    = lifetable_seer,   scenario     = "lifetime",   criterion    = "AIC" )  knitr::kable(result, digits = 5,              caption = "Monthly transition probabilities — Melanoma (lifetime, AIC)")

title: Introduction to seerSurv: Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities
Introduction to seerSurv: Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities
code: mods_L <- fit_models(ipd_L[-1, ])   # drop time = 0 row mods_D <- fit_models(ipd_D[-1, ])  names(mods_L) #> [1] "exp"      "weibull"  "gompertz" "llogis"   "lnorm"    "gamma"

title: Introduction to seerSurv: Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities
code: ic_L   <- extract_ic(mods_L, "AIC") wts_L  <- compute_weights(ic_L, top_k = 3, criterion = "AIC") wts_L #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   model    weight    ic criterion #>   <chr>     <dbl> <dbl> <chr>     #> 1 exp       0.576  2.27 AIC       #> 2 lnorm     0.212  4.26 AIC       #> 3 gompertz  0.212  4.26 AIC

title: Introduction to seerSurv: Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities
code: grid     <- seq(0, 39, by = 1)   # 39-year lifetime horizon from age 61 S_blend_L <- blend_survival(mods_L, wts_L, grid) #> New names: #> • `surv` -> `surv...1` #> • `surv` -> `surv...2` #> • `surv` -> `surv...3` head(S_blend_L) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>    time  surv #>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1     0 1     #> 2     1 0.996 #> 3     2 0.991 #> 4     3 0.987 #> 5     4 0.983 #> 6     5 0.979

title: Introduction to seerSurv: Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities
code: lt <- lifetable_seer |>   mutate(     btrate_LR = Males * 0.580 + Females * 0.420,     btrate_DR = Males * 0.692 + Females * 0.308   )  B_LR <- make_background_surv(lt, mean_age = 61, rate_col = "btrate_LR",                               time_grid_years = grid) B_DR <- make_background_surv(lt, mean_age = 62, rate_col = "btrate_DR",                               time_grid_years = grid) head(B_LR) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>    time  surv #>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1     0 1     #> 2     1 0.990 #> 3     2 0.979 #> 4     3 0.967 #> 5     4 0.955 #> 6     5 0.942

title: Introduction to seerSurv: Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities
text: Blended net survival curves — Melanoma
code: ic_D  <- extract_ic(mods_D, "AIC") wts_D <- compute_weights(ic_D, top_k = 3, criterion = "AIC") S_blend_D <- blend_survival(mods_D, wts_D, grid) #> New names: #> • `surv` -> `surv...1` #> • `surv` -> `surv...2` #> • `surv` -> `surv...3`  U_L <- left_join(S_blend_L, B_LR, by = "time") |>   mutate(surv = surv.x * surv.y) |> select(time, surv)  U_D <- left_join(S_blend_D, B_DR, by = "time") |>   mutate(surv = surv.x * surv.y) |> select(time, surv)  plot_survival_curves(S_blend_L, S_blend_D, U_L, U_D, tumour = "Melanoma")

title: Introduction to seerSurv: Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities
text: Monthly transition probabilities across 11 cancer types (lifetime, AIC)
code: data(tumour_data_seer)  results_multi <- tumour_data_seer |>   group_by(Tumor) |>   summarise(     out = list(       run_tumour_analysis(         tumour       = first(Tumor),         surv_vec_LR  = c(S0[Recurrence == "LR"], S1[Recurrence == "LR"],                          S2[Recurrence == "LR"], S3[Recurrence == "LR"],                          S4[Recurrence == "LR"], S5[Recurrence == "LR"]),         surv_vec_DR  = c(S0[Recurrence == "DR"], S1[Recurrence == "DR"],                          S2[Recurrence == "DR"], S3[Recurrence == "DR"],                          S4[Recurrence == "DR"], S5[Recurrence == "DR"]),         N_L          = first(N_LR),         N_D          = first(N_DR),         prop_male_LR = prop_male[Recurrence == "LR"],         mean_age_LR  = mean_age[Recurrence == "LR"],         prop_male_DR = prop_male[Recurrence == "DR"],         mean_age_DR  = mean_age[Recurrence == "DR"],         lifetable    = lifetable_seer,         scenario     = "lifetime",         criterion    = "AIC"       )     ),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   tidyr::unnest(out)  knitr::kable(   results_multi |> select(Tumor, P_LL, P_DL_approach2, P_Death_L_approach2,                            M_months),   digits  = 5,   caption = "Monthly transition probabilities across 11 cancer types (lifetime, AIC)" )

what: Sensitivity analysis: time horizons and model-selection criteria
title: Introduction to seerSurv: Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities
text: Sensitivity analysis — Melanoma across scenarios criteria
code: scenarios  <- c("lifetime", "20y", "5y") criteria   <- c("AIC", "BIC")  sens_grid <- expand.grid(scenario = scenarios, criterion = criteria,                          stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  sens_results <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(sens_grid)), function(i) {   run_tumour_analysis(     tumour       = "Melanoma",     surv_vec_LR  = s_lr,     surv_vec_DR  = s_dr,     N_L          = 64775L,     N_D          = 3121L,     prop_male_LR = 0.580,     mean_age_LR  = 61,     prop_male_DR = 0.692,     mean_age_DR  = 62,     lifetable    = lifetable_seer,     scenario     = sens_grid$scenario[i],     criterion    = sens_grid$criterion[i]   ) |>     mutate(scenario  = sens_grid$scenario[i],            criterion = sens_grid$criterion[i]) })  sens_df <- bind_rows(sens_results) |>   select(scenario, criterion, P_LL, P_DL_approach2, P_Death_L_approach2)  knitr::kable(sens_df, digits = 5,              caption = "Sensitivity analysis — Melanoma across scenarios and criteria")

title: seerSurv — Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities
text: seerSurv provides transparent, reproducible framework deriving monthly cancer-recurrence transition probabilities used state-transition health economic models. Given five-year aggregate survival proportions local/regional recurrence (LR) distant recurrence (DR) populations—typically extracted SEER-17 registry—package estimates: Bundled data cover 11 cancer types (Breast, Bladder, Colon & Rectum, Esophageal, Kidney, Liver, Lung, Melanoma, Ovarian, Pancreatic, Stomach).

title: seerSurv — Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities
code: # Install the released CRAN version (once available) install.packages("seerSurv")  # Install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("pharmacoevidence/seerSurv")

title: seerSurv — Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities
code: library(seerSurv)  data(lifetable_seer)  result <- run_tumour_analysis(   tumour       = "Melanoma",   surv_vec_LR  = c(1, 0.9959, 0.9886, 0.9831, 0.9783, 0.9754),   surv_vec_DR  = c(1, 0.5491, 0.4396, 0.3945, 0.3630, 0.3410),   N_L          = 64775L,   N_D          = 3121L,   prop_male_LR = 0.580,   mean_age_LR  = 61,   prop_male_DR = 0.692,   mean_age_DR  = 62,   lifetable    = lifetable_seer,   scenario     = "lifetime",   # "lifetime", "20y", or "5y"   criterion    = "AIC"         # "AIC" or "BIC" )  result #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>     P_LL P_DL_approach2 P_Death_L_approach2 RMST_L_years RMST_D_years M_years M_months #>    <dbl>          <dbl>               <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1  0.999         0.0003               4e-04         17.2         3.15    14.1     169.

title: seerSurv — Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities
code: library(dplyr) library(tidyr)  data(tumour_data_seer)  results <- tumour_data_seer |>   group_by(Tumor) |>   summarise(     out = list(       run_tumour_analysis(         tumour       = first(Tumor),         surv_vec_LR  = c(S0[Recurrence == "LR"], S1[Recurrence == "LR"],                          S2[Recurrence == "LR"], S3[Recurrence == "LR"],                          S4[Recurrence == "LR"], S5[Recurrence == "LR"]),         surv_vec_DR  = c(S0[Recurrence == "DR"], S1[Recurrence == "DR"],                          S2[Recurrence == "DR"], S3[Recurrence == "DR"],                          S4[Recurrence == "DR"], S5[Recurrence == "DR"]),         N_L          = first(N_LR),         N_D          = first(N_DR),         prop_male_LR = prop_male[Recurrence == "LR"],         mean_age_LR  = mean_age[Recurrence == "LR"],         prop_male_DR = prop_male[Recurrence == "DR"],         mean_age_DR  = mean_age[Recurrence == "DR"],         lifetable    = lifetable_seer,         scenario     = "lifetime",         criterion    = "AIC"       )     ),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   unnest(out)

title: seerSurv — Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities
text: P(LL) derived mean LR sojourn time (RMST differential).P(DL) estimated minimising relative sum--squares observed LR survival curve convolution approximation. Full details provided companion vignette associated publication.
code: Input: 5-year aggregate survival (LR and DR) + demographics      │      ▼  prep_ipd()           ← convert to pseudo-IPD (probability-weighted)      │      ▼  fit_models()         ← 6 parametric distributions (flexsurv)      │      ▼  extract_ic() +       ← select top-k by AIC / BIC  compute_weights()    ← assign relative-likelihood weights      │      ▼  blend_survival()     ← weighted-average survival curve      │      ▼  make_background_surv() ← multiply by US-CDC life-table survival      │      ▼  run_tumour_analysis()  ← optimise P(LL) and P(DL), return tibble

title: seerSurv — Cancer Recurrence Transition Probabilities

what: Blend Parametric Survival Curves Using Model-Averaging Weights — blend_survival
title: Blend Parametric Survival Curves Using Model-Averaging Weights — blend_survival
text: Produces single, weighted-average survival curve combining predicted survival probabilities top-\(k\) parametric models selected compute_weights.  blend convex combination: $$\hat{S}(t) = \sum_{=1}^{k} w_i \, S_i(t),$$ \(S_i(t)\) predicted survival \(\)-th model \(w_i\) relative-likelihood weights.

title: Blend Parametric Survival Curves Using Model-Averaging Weights — blend_survival
code: blend_survival(models, weights_tbl, times)

title: Blend Parametric Survival Curves Using Model-Averaging Weights — blend_survival
text: models named list fitted flexsurvreg objects, typically fit_models. weights_tbl tibble compute_weights columns model (matching names models) weight. times Numeric vector time points evaluate blended survival curve.

title: Blend Parametric Survival Curves Using Model-Averaging Weights — blend_survival
text: tibble two columns: time Time points (times). surv Blended survival probability.

title: Blend Parametric Survival Curves Using Model-Averaging Weights — blend_survival
code: # \donttest{ t <- 0:5 s <- c(1, 0.549, 0.440, 0.394, 0.363, 0.341) ipd  <- prep_ipd(t, s) mods <- fit_models(ipd[-1, ]) ic   <- extract_ic(mods, "AIC") wts  <- compute_weights(ic, top_k = 3, criterion = "AIC") grid <- seq(0, 10, by = 0.25) S_blend <- blend_survival(mods, wts, grid) #> New names: #> • `surv` -> `surv...1` #> • `surv` -> `surv...2` #> • `surv` -> `surv...3` head(S_blend) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>    time  surv #>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1  0    1     #> 2  0.25 0.976 #> 3  0.5  0.951 #> 4  0.75 0.925 #> 5  1    0.901 #> 6  1.25 0.876 # }

what: Compute Relative-Likelihood Weights for Model Averaging — compute_weights
title: Compute Relative-Likelihood Weights for Model Averaging — compute_weights
text: Selects top-\(k\) models information criterion value assigns relative-likelihood weights using Akaike / Schwarz weight formula: $$w_i = \frac{\exp\!\bigl((\mathrm{IC}_{\min} - \mathrm{IC}_i)/2\bigr)}              {\sum_{j=1}^{k} \exp\!\bigl((\mathrm{IC}_{\min} - \mathrm{IC}_j)/2\bigr)}.$$

title: Compute Relative-Likelihood Weights for Model Averaging — compute_weights
code: compute_weights(ic_vals, top_k = 3L, criterion = "AIC")

title: Compute Relative-Likelihood Weights for Model Averaging — compute_weights
text: ic_vals Named numeric vector information criterion values, e.g. extract_ic. top_k Integer.  Number top-ranked models retain (default 3). criterion Character scalar passed labelling . Typically "AIC" "BIC".

title: Compute Relative-Likelihood Weights for Model Averaging — compute_weights
text: tibble four columns: model Distribution name (names ic_vals). weight Relative-likelihood model weight (sums 1). ic Raw information criterion value. criterion Label passed via criterion argument.

title: Compute Relative-Likelihood Weights for Model Averaging — compute_weights
code: ic <- c(exp = 240.1, weibull = 238.7, gompertz = 241.5,         llogis = 239.3, lnorm = 243.0, gamma = 238.9) compute_weights(ic, top_k = 3, criterion = "AIC") #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   model   weight    ic criterion #>   <chr>    <dbl> <dbl> <chr>     #> 1 weibull  0.378  239. AIC       #> 2 gamma    0.342  239. AIC       #> 3 llogis   0.280  239. AIC

what: Extract Information Criteria from a List of Parametric Survival Models — extract_ic
title: Extract Information Criteria from a List of Parametric Survival Models — extract_ic
text: Extracts either Akaike Information Criterion (AIC) Bayesian Information Criterion (BIC) flexsurvreg object named list, returning named numeric vector use compute_weights.

title: Extract Information Criteria from a List of Parametric Survival Models — extract_ic
code: extract_ic(models, criterion = c("AIC", "BIC"))

title: Extract Information Criteria from a List of Parametric Survival Models — extract_ic
text: models named list fitted flexsurvreg objects, typically output fit_models. criterion Character scalar: "AIC" (default) "BIC".

title: Extract Information Criteria from a List of Parametric Survival Models — extract_ic
text: named numeric vector information criterion values, one per   model.

title: Extract Information Criteria from a List of Parametric Survival Models — extract_ic
code: # \donttest{ t <- 0:5 s <- c(1, 0.549, 0.440, 0.394, 0.363, 0.341) ipd   <- prep_ipd(t, s) mods  <- fit_models(ipd[-1, ]) extract_ic(mods, "AIC") #>      exp  weibull gompertz   llogis    lnorm    gamma  #> 3.375273 5.375252 5.356812 5.357385 5.331519 5.374326  extract_ic(mods, "BIC") #>       exp   weibull  gompertz    llogis     lnorm     gamma  #> 0.7756162 0.1759382 0.1574987 0.1580710 0.1322055 0.1750123  # }

what: Fit a Panel of Parametric Survival Models to Pseudo-IPD — fit_models
title: Fit a Panel of Parametric Survival Models to Pseudo-IPD — fit_models
text: Fits distribution named dists pseudo-individual patient data (pseudo-IPD) data frame using flexsurvreg observation weights.  resulting named list designed passed directly extract_ic blend_survival.

title: Fit a Panel of Parametric Survival Models to Pseudo-IPD — fit_models
code: fit_models(   ipd,   dists = c("exp", "weibull", "gompertz", "llogis", "lnorm", "gamma") )

title: Fit a Panel of Parametric Survival Models to Pseudo-IPD — fit_models
text: ipd tibble / data frame least three columns: time Event censoring time (numeric, > 0). event Event indicator (1 = event, 0 = censored). weight Observation weight (non-negative numeric). Typically output prep_ipd first row (time = 0) removed. dists Character vector distribution names accepted flexsurvreg.  Defaults six-distribution set used throughout seerSurv: c("exp", "weibull", "gompertz", "llogis", "lnorm", "gamma").

title: Fit a Panel of Parametric Survival Models to Pseudo-IPD — fit_models
text: named list flexsurvreg objects, one per distribution.   Names match corresponding element dists.

title: Fit a Panel of Parametric Survival Models to Pseudo-IPD — fit_models
text: ipd data frame first row (time = 0) removed calling function flexsurvreg requires strictly positive event times.  common idiom :
code: ipd  <- prep_ipd(time_vec, surv_vec)   mods <- fit_models(ipd[-1, ])

title: Fit a Panel of Parametric Survival Models to Pseudo-IPD — fit_models
code: # \donttest{ t <- 0:5 s <- c(1, 0.549, 0.440, 0.394, 0.363, 0.341)   # melanoma DR (SEER-17) ipd   <- prep_ipd(t, s) mods  <- fit_models(ipd[-1, ]) names(mods) #> [1] "exp"      "weibull"  "gompertz" "llogis"   "lnorm"    "gamma"    # }

what: US-CDC Life-Table for SEER-17 Background Mortality Adjustment — lifetable_seer
title: US-CDC Life-Table for SEER-17 Background Mortality Adjustment — lifetable_seer
text: Annual death rates single year age (0–100) sex US Centers Disease Control Prevention (CDC) National Center Health Statistics, aligned SEER-17 study period (cases diagnosed 2021).  Used make_background_surv run_tumour_analysis normalise relative (disease-specific) survival estimates net (-cause) survival.

title: US-CDC Life-Table for SEER-17 Background Mortality Adjustment — lifetable_seer
code: lifetable_seer

title: US-CDC Life-Table for SEER-17 Background Mortality Adjustment — lifetable_seer
text: data frame 101 rows 3 columns: Age Integer age (0, 1, 2, …, 100). Males Annual probability death males age. Females Annual probability death females age.

title: US-CDC Life-Table for SEER-17 Background Mortality Adjustment — lifetable_seer
text: US CDC/NCHS National Vital Statistics Reports, underlying cause--death life-tables (https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/nvss/life-tables.htm).

title: US-CDC Life-Table for SEER-17 Background Mortality Adjustment — lifetable_seer
text: sex-mixed rate cohort proportion male \(\pi\) constructed : $$q_{\text{mix}} = \pi \cdot q_{\text{males}} + (1 - \pi) \cdot q_{\text{females}}.$$ done inside run_tumour_analysis recurrence-type subgroup.

title: US-CDC Life-Table for SEER-17 Background Mortality Adjustment — lifetable_seer
code: data(lifetable_seer) head(lifetable_seer) #>   Age    Males  Females #> 1   0 0.006023 0.005132 #> 2   1 0.000481 0.000394 #> 3   2 0.000332 0.000232 #> 4   3 0.000237 0.000187 #> 5   4 0.000179 0.000142 #> 6   5 0.000165 0.000133 plot(lifetable_seer$Age, lifetable_seer$Males, type = "l",      xlab = "Age", ylab = "Annual death rate", main = "US-CDC Life-Table") lines(lifetable_seer$Age, lifetable_seer$Females, lty = 2) legend("topleft", c("Males", "Females"), lty = 1:2)

what: Construct Background (All-Cause) Survival from a US-CDC Life-Table — make_background_surv
title: Construct Background (All-Cause) Survival from a US-CDC Life-Table — make_background_surv
text: Uses age- sex-specific annual death rates life-table build cumulative background survival curve, interpolates onto user- supplied time grid.  resulting curve multiplied relative (disease-specific) survival curve inside run_tumour_analysis obtain net (-cause) survival.

title: Construct Background (All-Cause) Survival from a US-CDC Life-Table — make_background_surv
code: make_background_surv(lifetable, mean_age, rate_col, time_grid_years)

title: Construct Background (All-Cause) Survival from a US-CDC Life-Table — make_background_surv
text: lifetable data frame containing life-table.  Must include Age column (integer ages 0, 1, 2, …) least one numeric column whose name supplied via rate_col.  bundled lifetable_seer dataset canonical input. mean_age Numeric scalar.  mean (median) age study cohort.  life-table sub-set rows Age >= floor(mean_age). rate_col Character scalar.  Name column lifetable contains annual death rates use (e.g. "btrate_LR" "btrate_DR" sex-mixing applied). time_grid_years Numeric vector time points (years) onto cumulative survival interpolated.

title: Construct Background (All-Cause) Survival from a US-CDC Life-Table — make_background_surv
text: tibble two columns: time Time years (time_grid_years). surv Background survival probability time point.

title: Construct Background (All-Cause) Survival from a US-CDC Life-Table — make_background_surv
text: cumulative background survival integer year \(t\) (starting floor(mean_age)) : $$B(t) = \prod_{j=0}^{t-1} \bigl(1 - q_j\bigr),$$ \(q_j\) annual death rate age \(j + \lfloor\text{mean age}\rfloor\). Non-integer time points linearly interpolated; times beyond last life-table row use flat extrapolation (rule = 2 approx).

title: Construct Background (All-Cause) Survival from a US-CDC Life-Table — make_background_surv
code: data(lifetable_seer)  # Add a sex-mixed rate column (60% male cohort) lt <- lifetable_seer lt$btrate_mix <- lt$Males * 0.60 + lt$Females * 0.40  grid <- seq(0, 39, by = 1)   # 39-year horizon from age 61 B <- make_background_surv(lt, mean_age = 61, rate_col = "btrate_mix",                           time_grid_years = grid) head(B) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>    time  surv #>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1     0 1     #> 2     1 0.990 #> 3     2 0.978 #> 4     3 0.967 #> 5     4 0.954 #> 6     5 0.941

what: Plot Blended Survival Curves for LR and DR Populations — plot_survival_curves
title: Plot Blended Survival Curves for LR and DR Populations — plot_survival_curves
text: Produces ggplot comparing four survival curves: disease-specific blended LR, disease-specific blended DR, net LR, net DR.  Designed give quick visual QC check running blend_survival make_background_surv.

title: Plot Blended Survival Curves for LR and DR Populations — plot_survival_curves
code: plot_survival_curves(   S_blend_L,   S_blend_D,   U_L = NULL,   U_D = NULL,   tumour = "Tumour",   time_unit = "Years" )

title: Plot Blended Survival Curves for LR and DR Populations — plot_survival_curves
text: S_blend_L two-column tibble (time, surv) — blended disease-specific LR survival blend_survival. S_blend_D two-column tibble (time, surv) — blended disease-specific DR survival. U_L two-column tibble (time, surv) — net LR survival (disease-specific × background).  Pass NULL omit. U_D two-column tibble (time, surv) — net DR survival. Pass NULL omit. tumour Character scalar used plot title (default "Tumour"). time_unit Character scalar x-axis label (default "Years").

title: Plot Blended Survival Curves for LR and DR Populations — plot_survival_curves
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot Blended Survival Curves for LR and DR Populations — plot_survival_curves
code: # \donttest{ data(lifetable_seer)  t <- 0:5 s_lr <- c(1, 0.9959, 0.9886, 0.9831, 0.9783, 0.9754) s_dr <- c(1, 0.5491, 0.4396, 0.3945, 0.3630, 0.3410) grid <- seq(0, 39, by = 1)  ipd_L <- prep_ipd(t, s_lr) ipd_D <- prep_ipd(t, s_dr) mods_L <- fit_models(ipd_L[-1, ]) mods_D <- fit_models(ipd_D[-1, ]) wts_L  <- compute_weights(extract_ic(mods_L, "AIC"), 3, "AIC") wts_D  <- compute_weights(extract_ic(mods_D, "AIC"), 3, "AIC") SL     <- blend_survival(mods_L, wts_L, grid) #> New names: #> • `surv` -> `surv...1` #> • `surv` -> `surv...2` #> • `surv` -> `surv...3` SD     <- blend_survival(mods_D, wts_D, grid) #> New names: #> • `surv` -> `surv...1` #> • `surv` -> `surv...2` #> • `surv` -> `surv...3`  lt     <- lifetable_seer lt$btrate_LR <- lt$Males * 0.58 + lt$Females * 0.42 lt$btrate_DR <- lt$Males * 0.69 + lt$Females * 0.31 BL <- make_background_surv(lt, 61, "btrate_LR", grid) BD <- make_background_surv(lt, 62, "btrate_DR", grid)  UL <- dplyr::mutate(   dplyr::left_join(SL, BL, by = "time"),   surv = surv.x * surv.y )[, c("time", "surv")] UD <- dplyr::mutate(   dplyr::left_join(SD, BD, by = "time"),   surv = surv.x * surv.y )[, c("time", "surv")]  p <- plot_survival_curves(SL, SD, UL, UD, tumour = "Melanoma") p  # }

what: Predict Survival from a Single flexsurvreg Model — predict_surv
title: Predict Survival from a Single flexsurvreg Model — predict_surv
text: thin wrapper around summary.flexsurvreg extracts estimated survival probability requested time point. Intended internal use users want individual model curves blending.

title: Predict Survival from a Single flexsurvreg Model — predict_surv
code: predict_surv(model, times)

title: Predict Survival from a Single flexsurvreg Model — predict_surv
text: model fitted flexsurvreg object. times Numeric vector time points evaluate survival (units data used fit model).

title: Predict Survival from a Single flexsurvreg Model — predict_surv
text: Numeric vector survival estimates, one per element times.

title: Predict Survival from a Single flexsurvreg Model — predict_surv

what: Prepare Pseudo-Individual Patient Data from Aggregate Survival Proportions — prep_ipd
title: Prepare Pseudo-Individual Patient Data from Aggregate Survival Proportions — prep_ipd
text: Converts vector aggregate survival proportions (typically read Kaplan–Meier table registry publication) pseudo-individual patient data (pseudo-IPD) tibble suitable passing fit_models.  row represents one "pseudo-patient"; weight probability mass Kaplan–Meier curve drops time point.  last observed subject treated censored.

title: Prepare Pseudo-Individual Patient Data from Aggregate Survival Proportions — prep_ipd
code: prep_ipd(time, surv)

title: Prepare Pseudo-Individual Patient Data from Aggregate Survival Proportions — prep_ipd
text: time Numeric vector observed time points (years).  Must non-negative, strictly increasing, length surv. surv Numeric vector survival probabilities time point. Must start 1 (close ), non-increasing, lie \([0, 1]\).

title: Prepare Pseudo-Individual Patient Data from Aggregate Survival Proportions — prep_ipd
text: tibble three columns: time Time point (units input). weight Probability mass time point (non-negative,     sums 1). event Event indicator: 1 observed event,     0 last (censored) observation. Rows zero weight silently dropped.

title: Prepare Pseudo-Individual Patient Data from Aggregate Survival Proportions — prep_ipd
text: weight time \(t_i\) computed $$w_i = | S(t_i) - S(t_{+1}) |,$$ final weight adjusted weights sum 1.  ensures empirical distribution implied pseudo-IPD exactly reproduces input survival curve.

title: Prepare Pseudo-Individual Patient Data from Aggregate Survival Proportions — prep_ipd

what: Compute the Restricted Mean Survival Time (Trapezoidal Rule) — rmst
title: Compute the Restricted Mean Survival Time (Trapezoidal Rule) — rmst
text: Calculates area survival curve last observed time point using composite trapezoidal rule.  result equals restricted mean survival time (RMST) input curve evaluated sufficiently fine grid.

title: Compute the Restricted Mean Survival Time (Trapezoidal Rule) — rmst
code: rmst(time, surv)

title: Compute the Restricted Mean Survival Time (Trapezoidal Rule) — rmst
text: time Numeric vector time points (strictly increasing, units survival data). surv Numeric vector survival probabilities evaluated element time.  Must length time.

title: Compute the Restricted Mean Survival Time (Trapezoidal Rule) — rmst
text: single numeric value: area curve (RMST)   units time.

title: Compute the Restricted Mean Survival Time (Trapezoidal Rule) — rmst
text: trapezoidal approximation : $$\text{RMST} \approx \sum_{=1}^{n-1} (t_{+1} - t_i)   \cdot \frac{S(t_i) + S(t_{+1})}{2}.$$ uniform grids simplifies : $$\Delta t \cdot \Bigl[\tfrac{1}{2}S(t_0) + S(t_1) + \cdots +   S(t_{n-2}) + \tfrac{1}{2}S(t_{n-1})\Bigr].$$

title: Compute the Restricted Mean Survival Time (Trapezoidal Rule) — rmst
code: # RMST from a synthetic exponential curve t <- seq(0, 10, by = 0.1) s <- exp(-0.1 * t) rmst(t, s)   # close to 1/0.1 = 10 as horizon -> infinity #> [1] 6.321258

what: Estimate Cancer-Recurrence Transition Probabilities for One Tumour Type — run_tumour_analysis
title: Estimate Cancer-Recurrence Transition Probabilities for One Tumour Type — run_tumour_analysis
text: primary user-facing function seerSurv.  Given five-year aggregate survival proportions local/regional-recurrence (LR) distant- recurrence (DR) populations together demographic inputs, function: Converts aggregate survival proportions pseudo-IPD. Fits panel parametric models selects top-\(k\)         information criterion (AIC BIC). Blends selected models using relative-likelihood weights. Adjusts age- sex-specific background mortality         US-CDC life-tables. Computes mean LR sojourn time (RMST differential) derives         monthly LR persistence probability \(P(\text{LR})\). Estimates monthly LR--DR transition probability         \(P(\text{DR})\) via convolution optimisation. Returns three monthly transition probabilities:         \(P(\text{LR})\), \(P(\text{DR})\),         \(P(\text{Death} | \text{LR})\) = \(1 - P(\text{LR}) - P(\text{DR})\).

title: Estimate Cancer-Recurrence Transition Probabilities for One Tumour Type — run_tumour_analysis
code: run_tumour_analysis(   tumour,   surv_vec_LR,   surv_vec_DR,   N_L,   N_D,   prop_male_LR,   mean_age_LR,   prop_male_DR,   mean_age_DR,   lifetable,   scenario = c("lifetime", "20y", "5y"),   criterion = c("AIC", "BIC"),   dists = c("exp", "weibull", "gompertz", "llogis", "lnorm", "gamma"),   top_k = 3L,   max_age = 100L )

title: Estimate Cancer-Recurrence Transition Probabilities for One Tumour Type — run_tumour_analysis
text: tumour Character scalar.  Tumour label used identification multi-tumour analyses (e.g. "Melanoma"). surv_vec_LR Numeric vector survival probabilities LR population years 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 (must start 1). surv_vec_DR Numeric vector survival probabilities DR population years 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 (must start 1). N_L Integer.  Number LR patients SEER cohort (informational; currently stored provenance future sample-size adjustments). N_D Integer.  Number DR patients SEER cohort (informational). prop_male_LR Numeric \([0, 1]\).  Proportion male LR population, used construct sex-mixed background mortality rate. mean_age_LR Numeric.  Mean (median) age LR population. prop_male_DR Numeric \([0, 1]\).  Proportion male DR population. mean_age_DR Numeric.  Mean (median) age DR population. lifetable data frame columns Age, Males, Females containing annual death rates.  bundled lifetable_seer dataset canonical choice. scenario Character scalar controlling extrapolation horizon. One : "lifetime" Lifetime horizon: \(100 - \min(\text{mean age LR}, \text{mean age DR})\) years. "20y" Fixed 20-year horizon. "5y" Fixed 5-year horizon. criterion Character scalar: "AIC" (default) "BIC". Governs model selection weight computation. dists Character vector distribution names passed fit_models.  Defaults six-distribution base set. top_k Integer.  Number top-ranked models include blend (default 3). max_age Integer.  Maximum age assumed lifetime horizon calculation (default 100).

title: Estimate Cancer-Recurrence Transition Probabilities for One Tumour Type — run_tumour_analysis
text: one-row tibble containing: P_LL Monthly probability remaining LR. P_DL_approach2 Monthly probability transitioning LR     DR (convolution optimisation, Approach 2). P_Death_L_approach2 Monthly probability death LR     (\(1 - P_{\text{LL}} - P_{\text{DL}}\)). RMST_L_years Net RMST LR population (years). RMST_D_years Net RMST DR population (years). M_years Mean LR sojourn time (RMST differential, years). M_months Mean LR sojourn time (months).

title: Estimate Cancer-Recurrence Transition Probabilities for One Tumour Type — run_tumour_analysis
text: mean LR sojourn time \(M\) (months) area net LR net DR survival curves.  \(P(\text{LR})\) root : $$\frac{1 - p^{N+1}}{1 - p} = M,$$ \(N\) total number monthly time steps horizon.

title: Estimate Cancer-Recurrence Transition Probabilities for One Tumour Type — run_tumour_analysis
text: modelled LR survival \(R(t)\) approximated sum patients still LR (\(L(t) = P_{\text{LL}}^t\)) progressed DR: $$R(t) \approx L(t) + P_{\text{DL}} \sum_{=0}^{t-1} L()\, Q_D(t-),$$ \(Q_D(\cdot)\) monthly DR survival.  \(P_{\text{DL}}\) estimated minimising relative sum--squares discrepancy \(R(t)\) right-hand side \(t = 1, \ldots, N\).

title: Estimate Cancer-Recurrence Transition Probabilities for One Tumour Type — run_tumour_analysis
code: # \donttest{ data(lifetable_seer)  result <- run_tumour_analysis(   tumour       = "Melanoma",   surv_vec_LR  = c(1, 0.9959, 0.9886, 0.9831, 0.9783, 0.9754),   surv_vec_DR  = c(1, 0.5491, 0.4396, 0.3945, 0.3630, 0.3410),   N_L          = 64775L,   N_D          = 3121L,   prop_male_LR = 0.580,   mean_age_LR  = 61,   prop_male_DR = 0.692,   mean_age_DR  = 62,   lifetable    = lifetable_seer,   scenario     = "lifetime",   criterion    = "AIC" ) #> New names: #> • `surv` -> `surv...1` #> • `surv` -> `surv...2` #> • `surv` -> `surv...3` #> New names: #> • `surv` -> `surv...1` #> • `surv` -> `surv...2` #> • `surv` -> `surv...3` result #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>    P_LL P_DL_approach2 P_Death_L_approach2 RMST_L_years RMST_D_years M_years #>   <dbl>          <dbl>               <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 0.993        0.00659           0.0000467         21.2         9.18    12.0 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: M_months <dbl> # }

what: seerSurv: Cancer Progression Transition Probabilities from Aggregate Survival Data — seerSurv-package
title: seerSurv: Cancer Progression Transition Probabilities from Aggregate Survival Data — seerSurv-package
text: seerSurv package provides transparent, reproducible framework estimating monthly transition probabilities used state-transition health economic models include local/regional recurrence (LR) state. Survival data LR distant-recurrence (DR) populations—typically extracted SEER-17 registry—extrapolated using weighted blend parametric survival models.  Age- sex-specific background mortality US-CDC life-tables layered top produce net (-cause) survival curves.  area differential LR DR curves yields mean LR sojourn time, \(P(\text{LR})\) derived.  convolution optimisation recovers \(P(\text{DR})\).

title: seerSurv: Cancer Progression Transition Probabilities from Aggregate Survival Data — seerSurv-package
text: Prepare pseudo-individual patient data aggregate survival         proportions prep_ipd. Fit panel parametric models fit_models. Select weight top-\(k\) models AIC BIC using         extract_ic compute_weights. Blend weighted survival curves blend_survival. Adjust background mortality         make_background_surv. Run complete analysis pipeline one many tumour types         run_tumour_analysis.

title: seerSurv: Cancer Progression Transition Probabilities from Aggregate Survival Data — seerSurv-package
text: lifetable_seer US-CDC annual death-rate life-table (ages 0–100) separate     columns males females, aligned SEER-17 study period. tumour_data_seer Reference 5-year aggregate survival proportions (years 0–5) LR     DR populations across 11 cancer types derived SEER-17, together     sample sizes, sex proportions, mean ages.

title: seerSurv: Cancer Progression Transition Probabilities from Aggregate Survival Data — seerSurv-package
text: Mansoori S, Pandey S, Rani R, Singh B, Kurt M (2025). "Deriving Cancer Progression Rates Local/Regional Recurrence Using Aggregate Survival Data: R Package Health Economic Evaluations." Value Health (submitted). Surveillance, Epidemiology, End Results (SEER) Program (https://seer.cancer.gov/) SEER*Stat Database.

title: seerSurv: Cancer Progression Transition Probabilities from Aggregate Survival Data — seerSurv-package
text: Sameer Mansoori, Shubhram Pandey, Rashi Rani, Barinder Singh, Murat Kurt Maintainer: Shubhram Pandey shubhram.pandey@heorlytics.com

what: SEER-17 Five-Year Aggregate Survival Data for 11 Cancer Types — tumour_data_seer
title: SEER-17 Five-Year Aggregate Survival Data for 11 Cancer Types — tumour_data_seer

Also includes sample sizes, proportion male, mean age subgroup.

title: SEER-17 Five-Year Aggregate Survival Data for 11 Cancer Types — tumour_data_seer
code: tumour_data_seer

title: SEER-17 Five-Year Aggregate Survival Data for 11 Cancer Types — tumour_data_seer

S2 Survival year 2. S3 Survival year 3. S4 Survival year 4. S5 Survival year 5. prop_male Proportion patients male. mean_age Mean (median) age recurrence. N_LR Number LR patients SEER cohort. N_DR Number DR patients SEER cohort.

text: 11 tumour types : Breast, Bladder, Colon & Rectum, Esophageal, Kidney, Liver, Lung, Melanoma, Ovarian, Pancreatic, Stomach.

title: SEER-17 Five-Year Aggregate Survival Data for 11 Cancer Types — tumour_data_seer
code: data(tumour_data_seer) dplyr::filter(tumour_data_seer, Tumor == "Melanoma") #> # A tibble: 2 × 12 #>   Tumor  Recurrence    S0    S1    S2    S3    S4    S5 prop_male mean_age  N_LR #>   <chr>  <chr>      <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl> <int> #> 1 Melan… LR             1 0.996 0.989 0.983 0.978 0.975     0.58        61 64501 #> 2 Melan… DR             1 0.549 0.440 0.394 0.363 0.341     0.692       62 64501 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: N_DR <int>

title: seerSurv 0.1.0 (2026-03-21)
text: run_tumour_analysis(): Primary wrapper function. Estimates monthly LR transition probabilities aggregate survival data. Supports three time horizons ("lifetime", "20y", "5y") two model-selection criteria ("AIC", "BIC"). prep_ipd(): Converts aggregate survival proportions probability-weighted pseudo-individual patient data. fit_models(): Fits panel six parametric distributions (exponential, Weibull, Gompertz, log-logistic, log-normal, gamma) pseudo-IPD via flexsurv. extract_ic(): Extracts AIC BIC named list flexsurvreg models. compute_weights(): Selects top-k models assigns relative-likelihood model-averaging weights. blend_survival(): Produces weighted-average blended survival curve. predict_surv(): Wrapper predict survival arbitrary time points single flexsurvreg model. make_background_surv(): Constructs background (-cause) survival age- sex-specific US-CDC life-table rates. rmst(): Restricted mean survival time (trapezoidal rule). plot_survival_curves(): ggplot2-based visualisation four survival curves (blended LR, blended DR, net LR, net DR).

title: seerSurv 0.1.0 (2026-03-21)
text: lifetable_seer: US-CDC annual death rates single year age sex (ages 0–100), aligned SEER-17 study period. tumour_data_seer: SEER-17 five-year aggregate survival proportions 11 cancer types (Breast, Bladder, Colon & Rectum, Esophageal, Kidney, Liver, Lung, Melanoma, Ovarian, Pancreatic, Stomach).
